title: Options, Payoffs & No-Arbitrage
description: Two portfolios with the same payoff must cost the same — so you can price and hedge any option by the trade that cannot lose. From the piecewise-linear payoff, through put-call parity and no-arbitrage bounds, to replication, the risk-neutral measure q, the binomial tree, and the delta hedge, with Black-Scholes reached as the continuous limit but never graded.
persona: For the quant-interview audience that knows expected value, combinatorics, and covariance and wants the finance payoff: pricing and hedging built entirely on exact, no-arbitrage reasoning. Every graded answer is an exact rational; Black-Scholes and the continuous Greeks are shown only as the continuous limit.
tagline: Price the trade that cannot lose.

lessons.title: Payoffs & the Contract
lessons.summary: Read and compose option payoffs: long/short calls and puts as piecewise-linear max(·,0); intrinsic vs time value; the protective-put identity max(S_T,K).

lessons.title: Put-Call Parity & No-Arbitrage
lessons.summary: Derive C − P = S − K·D as a no-arbitrage identity; spot the arbitrage gap, build the conversion trade, and bound a call or put by no-arbitrage.

lessons.title: Spreads & Straddles
lessons.summary: Compose multi-leg payoffs (bull spread, butterfly, straddle, strangle); read caps and break-points; bound a spread's price 0 ≤ c₁−c₂ ≤ K₂−K₁ by no-arbitrage.

lessons.title: One-Step Binomial: Price by Replication
lessons.summary: Price by replication (Δ shares + bond B) and by the risk-neutral measure q=(R−d)/(u−d); show hedge cost = price = (1/R)·E_q[payoff].

lessons.title: Multi-Step Binomial → Black-Scholes
lessons.summary: Fold a multi-step tree by backward induction; weight nodes nCk·q^k(1−q)^(n−k); reach Black-Scholes as the display-only continuous limit.

lessons.title: Synthesis: Hedging, Greeks & In the Wild
lessons.summary: The option delta is the Cov/Var hedge ratio; read the exact Greek signs; choose the right tool (delta-hedge vs replicate vs q-price) on unlabeled problems.
